title: ledum palustre subsp. decumbens
intro: Ledum palustre subsp. decumbens, also known as decumbent Labrador tea, is a subspecies of the Ledum palustre plant, a type of evergreen shrub native to North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Ledum palustre subsp. decumbens include its ability to boost the immune system, fight off infections, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to the affected area.
